meta.name: Dog Shelter design system
meta.source: Reference landing and dog profile mockups (warm shelter brand: DOG SHELTER)
meta.intent: High-level tokens and component guidelines for structure, spacing, typography, color, and interaction—use with Tailwind/CSS variables or design tools.

visualPillars: Organic rounded geometry (circles, ovals, pills, arches)—soft, friendly silhouette language
Generous whitespace; centered hero hierarchy; four-corner anchoring for chrome (logo, nav, contact, social)
Warm cream base + burnt-orange accent for emphasis and CTAs
Photography-forward: cut-out or contained pet imagery as focal hero elements
High contrast between accent orange and light backgrounds for accessibility of key actions
Editorial left rail (metadata + secondary cards) paired with a photographic hero on detail pages
Canonical marketing dog profile: centered arch card as focal point, mirrored metadata columns, generous negative space, glass overlay and soft elevation

primary.usage: Headings, logo wordmark, primary CTAs, key values, icons

primaryAlt.usage: Acceptable variant in same family for hover or large fills

secondaryText.usage: Labels, secondary nav, supporting copy (warm grey-brown)

body.usage: Body and description text on light backgrounds

decorative.usage: Large decorative shapes (paw pads, ovals behind photos)

glass.usage: Frosted cards: use at ~70–85% opacity with blur

onPrimary.usage: Text and icons on orange buttons and filled circles

interactive.primaryShadow: Diffuse orange glow (same hue, low saturation, large blur)

display.role: Hero titles, major headings, logo-adjacent emphasis
display.character: Rounded geometric sans; friendly, soft terminals

ui.character: Clean geometric sans; strong legibility at small sizes

dogNameArch.alignment: center above or integrated with arch hero on marketing dog profile mockups

logoWordmark.layout: Stacked or compact lockup beside circular mark

rules: Pair display font for emotional headlines with ui font for structure and density.
Uppercase labels are always small-caps scale with increased letter-spacing; never use all-caps for long paragraphs.
Orange is for emphasis—headlines that sell emotion, CTAs, and key facts; body copy stays dark neutral.

scale.section: 4–6rem vertical between major regions on marketing pages

layout.maxContentWidth: 72–80rem for main grid; hero may be full-bleed with internal max-width

grid.profileThreeColumn: Canonical marketing layout (15-06-10 reference): equal outer columns ~min 10–14rem flanking a flexible center arch card; left metadata + right metadata mirror. Also usable as legacy alternate beside app-specific rail layouts.
grid.dogDetailDesktop: At lg+ (~1024px): two columns—narrow left rail (minmax ~14–22rem) and flexible center. Left rail: all dog metadata fields in one vertical stack (left-aligned), then the AI overview card (same rail width, min-w-0 for streamed text). Center column: arch hero + glass adopt panel only—no overview beside the photo. Use one horizontal gap token (e.g. 2rem) between rail and hero.
grid.dogDetailMobile: Below lg: hero FIRST (order-1 via CSS), then metadata in a compact 2-col grid (sm: 3-col), then overview card, then chat. The dog photo is the emotional hook — never bury it below metadata on small screens. A display-weight dog-name heading appears above the hero on mobile (hidden on lg where the name is in the rail).
grid.dogDetailBelowHero: No separate Prev/Next text strip above chat. Full-width AI chat card directly under the two-column band.
grid.cornerAnchors: Logo top-left, primary nav top-right, contact block bottom-left, social or pagination bottom-right

symmetry: Single left rail: consistent vertical gap between metadata fields and between the last field and the AI overview card; all left-aligned.
Top-align the first metadata row with the top of the arch hero where possible (avoid asymmetric top padding on the rail vs hero).
AI overview card shares corner radius, border weight, and shadow language with other cards; rail uses min-width 0 so long streamed text wraps.

Hero sequence chevrons (dogHeroSequenceNav) are optically symmetric: same control size, mirrored inset from the left/right edges of the photo mask, at ~45% from top. Decorative FABs sit higher at ~15% near the arch curve. This separation avoids visual collision. Chevrons use frosted white/translucent fill; FABs use filled primary. z-index: both above image, chevrons actionable (pointer-events-auto), FABs decorative (pointer-events-none).
rhythm: Use one vertical spacing step between the two-column band and the full-width chat (e.g. 2rem) so the page reads as rail+hero then conversation.
Card headers: consistent horizontal padding and title size across AI overview and Ask about {dog}.

principles: Prioritize breathing room around hero and CTAs; avoid crowding the paw/arch focal shapes.
Align label/value stacks vertically with consistent gap (e.g. 0.25–0.5rem between label and value).
Use vertical rhythm: section gaps are multiples of base spacing unit.
On dog detail at lg+, treat left rail + hero as the primary band; chat is full-width and visually secondary below.

radius.pill: 9999px (full) for primary CTAs and circular icon buttons
radius.archTop: 50% width as radius or explicit large border-top radii for profile hero cards
radius.circle: 50% for logo icon container, social wells, floating FABs

principles: Default to soft corners everywhere; sharp rectangles are the exception.
Decorative blobs (paw pads, ovals) are organic—not perfect circles except for small UI controls.

shadows.cta: Soft diffuse shadow under primary buttons; hue-shifted toward brand orange, large blur, low opacity
shadows.floatingFab: Similar soft shadow on circular orange actions overlapping hero imagery
shadows.card: Very subtle neutral shadow optional; prefer separation via surface color and spacing

glassmorphism.usage: Description panels overlapping imagery on profile view
glassmorphism.recipe: Semi-transparent white or warm cream (roughly 70–85% opacity), backdrop blur (8–16px), light border or hairline optional, inner padding generous
glassmorphism.dogProfileOverlay: On the reference dog card, body copy in the glass panel may be centered for a calm, editorial feel; CTA remains the dominant interactive element below or within the overlay

siteHeader.structure: Flex row: logo lockup left, navigation cluster right
siteHeader.logo: Orange circle with white dog silhouette + stacked DOG SHELTER wordmark in brand orange
siteHeader.nav: Two-tier: small uppercase grey label above larger orange bold link (e.g. ABOUT US / WHO WE ARE)
siteHeader.behavior: Minimal chrome; text-only links without heavy underlines; hover may lighten orange or add subtle underline

heroLanding.structure: Centered composition; large decorative paw made of beige ovals with embedded puppy photography; main pad holds headline + subtitle + CTA
heroLanding.imagery: Cut-out or masked photos inside oval cells; consistent lighting and warm tone preferred
heroLanding.ctaPlacement: Below hero copy, centered on pad

buttonPrimary.trailingAffordance: Optional small circular inset with arrow icon (slightly darker orange fill)

buttonSecondary.usage: PREV / secondary navigation; de-emphasized vs NEXT

buttonPagination.prev: Light cream fill, dark text—paired sizing with NEXT
buttonPagination.note: On dog detail, prefer dogHeroSequenceNav (icon chevrons on the arch photo) instead of text Prev/Next pills; keep pill pattern for other flows (e.g. landing footer) if needed.

footerCluster.contact: Mirror nav pattern: CONTACT label (small grey uppercase) + GET IN TOUCH (orange bold)
footerCluster.social: Circular wells with FB / IG monograms in orange on muted circle background
footerCluster.alignment: Contact bottom-left, social bottom-right on landing; dog detail uses hero chevrons for sequence navigation, not footer pagination

dogProfileHeroCard.reference: High-fidelity centered dog profile (see Screenshot_From_2026-04-06_15-06-10): single-viewport desktop, warm cream canvas, premium minimal chrome.

composition.hierarchy: One dominant vertical profile unit in the center; metadata and page chrome frame it symmetrically. Heavy negative space around the card reads calm and high-end.
composition.outerShell: Large vertical profile container: light semi-transparent cream feel, very soft diffuse shadow (neutral or slight warm tint), large corner radii on any outer wrapper; arch cut at top of photo region.
composition.profileTitle: Dog name centered above the arch or at the crown of the composition in display font, large scale (see typography.scale.dogNameArch), brand.primary.
composition.photoRegion: High-quality photo centered inside the arch mask; object-cover; warm natural lighting aligns with palette.
composition.floatingActions: Heart (left) and paw (right) on the upper arch—white glyph on filled brand.primary circles, soft glow or shelter-class shadow so they float above the photo.

metadataColumns.marketingLayout: Two flanking columns with equal visual weight: left stack e.g. NAME, BREED, AGE (labels small muted uppercase; values larger display-weight brand.primary). Right stack e.g. COLOR (or coat), PERSONALITY, WEIGHT (or SIZE)—mirror spacing and alignment with left column (left column flush left, right column flush right of center card).
metadataColumns.dataModelNote: Map product fields to this pattern where sensible (e.g. SIZE/SEX/STATUS can replace or complement personality/weight columns); preserve label-above-value rhythm and orange values.
metadataColumns.implementationNote: The live app may use a single left rail plus AI column for density; when aligning to this reference, reintroduce mirrored side columns or center the name on the arch for marketing parity.

descriptionPanel.structure: Frosted glass rounded rectangle overlapping the lower third of the photo; generous padding; optional centered body text for the marketing variant.
descriptionPanel.content: Short emotional description in neutral.body; line length comfortable (~40–60ch if left-aligned; centered lines shorter).
descriptionPanel.cta: Primary pill 'Adopt this Puppy' (or equivalent) with trailing circular arrow affordance; high contrast; slight hover scale acceptable.

pageChrome.header: Logo top-left; two-tier nav top-right (small grey label + large orange link), matching siteHeader.
pageChrome.footerBand: Contact cluster bottom-left (CONTACT / GET IN TOUCH); bottom-right may hold PREV and NEXT as light cream pills with orange or dark text for dog sequence—alternative to in-photo chevron nav (see dogHeroSequenceNav vs buttonPagination).

dogProfileHeroCard.singleViewport: Target all profile-relevant content visible without vertical scroll on standard desktop height; outer margins and internal padding trade off against arch height.

dogDetailAiLayout.intent: Large screens: AI overview sits in the left rail below all metadata; hero column is photo + glass adopt only. Chat is full-width below the rail+hero band—no text pagination strip.
dogDetailAiLayout.breakpoints: At lg (~1024px): grid with left rail + hero; below lg stack metadata, overview, hero, then chat.
dogDetailAiLayout.overviewColumn: Card titled 'AI overview' with primary 'Generate' in the left rail under metadata; left accent border (border-l-[3px] brand.primary at 30% opacity) for visual distinction from generic cards; body scrolls inside a max height; min-w-0.
dogDetailAiLayout.chatColumn: Separate card 'Ask about {name}'; same card radius and border as overview; conversation area inset with subtle surface tint.
dogDetailAiLayout.sequenceNavigation: See dogHeroSequenceNav—chevron controls on the arch image, not paginationStrip.

dogHeroSequenceNav.placement: Flanking the arch photo mask (inside or slightly overlapping the image bounds), not below the overview card.
dogHeroSequenceNav.appearance: Icon-only chevrons (~20px stroke-width-2); circular hit target ~44px; frosted white fill (bg-white/80, backdrop-blur-sm), hairline border (border-white/50), subtle card shadow. 200ms hover: scale-110, increased opacity, shelter-sm shadow. Both prev and next use the same glass treatment for symmetry.
dogHeroSequenceNav.behavior: Links to previous/next dog by id; when no neighbor, control is visually disabled (reduced opacity, no navigation) with appropriate aria-disabled / non-interactive pattern.
dogHeroSequenceNav.accessibility: aria-label 'Previous dog' / 'Next dog'; visible focus ring (ring-2 ring-primary ring-offset-2 or equivalent).

dogCardList.note: Not fully shown in references; extend principles: rounded image top, cream/off-white card, orange accent for name or status, generous padding, soft shadow optional

formElements.note: Not shown; align inputs with ui font, rounded-md to rounded-xl borders, focus ring in brand primary

icons.style: Simple white glyphs on orange circles for primary actions; social as letter monograms in circle wells
icons.stroke: Prefer filled shapes or bold strokes readable at small sizes

principles: Subtle, reassuring transitions (150–250ms) on hover/focus for buttons and links
Optional gentle scale (1.02) on primary CTA hover—avoid aggressive bounce

contrast: Verify WCAG AA for orange text on cream (large headings may pass; small text may need darker orange or brown)
Ensure focus states are visible (ring or outline) on all interactive components
accessibility.touchTargets: Minimum ~44px for circular FABs and primary buttons

implementationNotes.tailwind: Map palette to CSS variables in :root and reference in tailwind.config theme.extend.colors
implementationNotes.nextImage: Remote dog photos allowed from configured hosts; prefer consistent aspect ratios inside oval/arch masks
implementationNotes.darkMode: Reference designs are light-first; if added, invert to deep warm grey background and desaturate orange slightly for legibility
